description: Two related config hygiene fixes: (1) config show leaks internal fields (telemetryNoticeShown, version) and raw-formats legacy fields (localPluginDirs, additionalAllowedDirectories) in user-visible output; (2) the MCP server warns 'Unknown config key' for telemetryNoticeShown and port even though the CLI writes them — add them to KNOWN_CONFIG_KEYS.

userStories.title: Hide internal fields and format legacy fields in config show
userStories.description: opentabs config show displays telemetryNoticeShown true and version 3 — these are internal implementation details. It also shows localPluginDirs [] and additionalAllowedDirectories [] as raw key=value lines when those fields appear in the config (from migrations). Hide the internal fields and add proper formatted sections for localPluginDirs and additionalAllowedDirectories similar to how localPlugins is handled.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs config show output does not contain 'telemetryNoticeShown' or 'version 3'
When config has localPluginDirs: ['path1'], config show displays it as a formatted 'localPluginDirs' section with '- path1' entries, not as raw 'localPluginDirs ["path1"]'
When config has additionalAllowedDirectories: ['path1'], config show displays it as a formatted 'additionalAllowedDirectories' section, not raw
Empty localPluginDirs and additionalAllowedDirectories arrays are NOT shown at all (no '(none)' entry for these — they are optional fields)
opentabs config show --json output still includes all fields including telemetryNoticeShown and version (JSON is for programmatic use)

userStories.title: Add telemetryNoticeShown and port to server KNOWN_CONFIG_KEYS
userStories.description: The MCP server logs 'Unknown config key "telemetryNoticeShown" in config.json' on every start. The CLI writes telemetryNoticeShown and port to config.json, but the server's KNOWN_CONFIG_KEYS set doesn't include them. Add both to the set so the server stops warning about CLI-written keys.
acceptanceCriteria: Server startup no longer logs 'Unknown config key "telemetryNoticeShown"' when config.json has that key
Server startup no longer logs 'Unknown config key "port"' when config.json has that key
Server still logs a warning for genuinely unknown keys (e.g., a typo like 'permisisons')
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts

Context:
KNOWN_CONFIG_KEYS (lines 163-171) currently has 7 keys:
```typescript
const KNOWN_CONFIG_KEYS = new Set([
  'version',
  'localPlugins',
  'localPluginDirs',
  'permissions',
  'settings',
  'additionalAllowedDirectories',
  'updateCheckIntervalMinutes',
]);
```
The CLI writes 'telemetryNoticeShown' (boolean) and 'port' (number) to config.json, but these are not in the server's set. Add them:
```typescript
const KNOWN_CONFIG_KEYS = new Set([
  'version',
  'localPlugins',
  'localPluginDirs',
  'permissions',
  'settings',
  'additionalAllowedDirectories',
  'updateCheckIntervalMinutes',
  'telemetryNoticeShown',
  'port',
]);
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the parseConfigRecord function behavior for these keys — the server intentionally ignores them (they're for CLI use only)
- The server does not need to read or use telemetryNoticeShown or port — just stop warning about them
